expectation: Run the full correction orchestrator. Remove the comparison because leading line breaks and repeated whitespace do not represent spoken-word errors.
originalText: A house fire happened in Gisborne on George Street in the Mangapapa area. Emergency services arrived early in the morning and quickly put out the fire. No one was injured, but the house was badly damaged. Police are trying to find out what caused the fire and think it might have been intentional. They said one person is helping with the investigation. This event reminds local residents how important it is to stay safe and watch out for each other.
userText:
A house fire happened in Gisborne on George Street, in the Mangapapa area. 

Emergency services arrived early in the morning and quickly put out the fire. No one was injured, but the house was badly damaged. Police are trying to find out what cause they fire and thinking it might had been intentional. They said one person is helping with the investigation. 

This event reminds local residents how important it is to stay safe and watch out for each other.

expectedFinalComparisons.studentPhrase: The original uses the verb phrase "caused the" to show what started the fire, while "cause they" changes both the verb form and the small word so the meaning is not correct.
The original uses the base form "think" after "and" in the same tense as "are trying," but "thinking" is a continuous form and does not match this structure.
The original uses "might have been" for a past possibility, while "might had been" is not a correct verb form in English.

expectation: Run the full correction orchestrator. Remove the comparison because smart and straight apostrophes represent the same word.
originalText: Kate Ceberano is a well-known singer. She has started to enjoy embroidery and quilting. She says her heritage from Hawaii and the Philippines inspired her. Every day, she works with fabric. She sews with a needle and adds beads and sequins to her work. One quilt she made is called Gaudi. It is based on her painting and took almost a year to finish. Even with her busy music schedule, Kate finds time each day to work on her quilts. She says making quilts is relaxing and helps her share her feelings. This craft was once passed in families. Now, many young artists like it. They use it to tell stories and stay calm. Kate’s love for textile art shows a side of her many people do not see.
userText: Kate Ceberano is a well-known singer. She has started to enjoy brodery and quilting, she says a hair teacher from a while and the Philippines inspired her. Everyday she works with fabric, she saws with the needle and adds bits and sequence to her work. One quilt she made is called 'Gaudi' is it based on her painting and took almost a year to finish even with her busy music schedule. Kate finds time each day to work on her quilts. She says making quilts is relaxing and helps her share her feelings. This craft was once passed in  families. Now many artist like it, they use it to tell stories and stay calm. Kate's love for textile are a chose aside for many people do not see

expectedFinalComparisons.studentPhrase: "Embroidery" is the correct spelling for this sewing craft; "brodery" is a misspelling.

expectedFinalComparisons.userText: a hair teacher from a while
expectedFinalComparisons.studentPhrase: The original phrase "her heritage from Hawaii" talks about her cultural background, while "a hair teacher from a while" changes the words and the meaning completely.
"Every day" (two words) means each day, while "everyday" (one word) is an adjective meaning ordinary.
"Sews" means to stitch with a needle and thread, but "saws" means to cut with a saw, so it does not fit the fabric context.
The original uses the indefinite article "a needle" to introduce one needle, while "the needle" sounds like a specific needle already known.
"Beads" are small decorative pieces used in sewing, while "bits" is too general and does not match the craft vocabulary here.
"Sequins" are small shiny decorations for fabric, while "sequence" is a different word meaning an order of things.
The original statement is "It is based on her painting," but "is it based" changes the word order into a question form.
The original says "young artists" (plural) to mean many people, while "artist" is singular and does not agree with "many".

expectedFinalComparisons.originalText: art shows a side of her
expectedFinalComparisons.studentPhrase: The original phrase "art shows a side of her" means the art reveals part of her personality, while "are a chose aside for" does not form a clear or correct English idea.

expectation: Run the full correction orchestrator. Remove the comparison because cozy and cosy are accepted regional spellings of the same word.
originalText: Some of the most stunning log homes can be found in places like Colorado and California. One charming house in Colorado sits on a peninsula surrounded by a lake, featuring huge log beams and a cozy antler chandelier. It comes with a guesthouse, a barn, and even a greenhouse dome, all on over forty-five acres of land. Meanwhile, on the California coast, a log house built in the style of a mountain cabin has a dramatic two-story living room with a large stone fireplace and a staircase made of logs. These unique homes showcase the beauty and craftsmanship of traditional log construction, making them truly special places to live or visit.
userText: Some of the stunning log homes can be found in places like Colorado and California. One charming house in Colorado sets on a penninsula surrounded by a lake, featuring huge log beams and a cosy antler chandelier. It comes with a guessed house, a barnand even a green house stone. All an over forty-five hecars of land. Meanwhile on the California coast a log house built in a style of a mountain cabin, has a dramatic two store, living room with a large stone fireplace and a staircase made of logs. These unique homes showcase the beauty and crossmentship of traditional log construction,making them truly special places to live or visit

expectedFinalComparisons.studentPhrase: The original uses "most stunning" to show the highest degree, while "stunning" alone is less strong.
The verb in the original is "sits" (meaning is located), while "sets" usually means puts or places something down.
"Peninsula" is the correct spelling; "penninsula" has extra letters.
The original has "guesthouse, a barn, and" as one compound noun and a separate "and", while "guessed house, a barnand" changes the word meaning and joins words incorrectly.
"Greenhouse dome" is one structure, but "green house stone" splits the compound noun and changes the meaning completely.
The original uses the preposition "on" ("on over forty-five acres"), while "an" is an article and does not fit this structure.
"Acres" is the correct word for land area; "hecars" is not an English word.
The original says "built in the style" (a specific style), while "built in a style" sounds more general.
"Story" is the correct word for a level of a building; "store" usually means a shop and is spelled differently for a different meaning.
"Craftsmanship" is the correct spelling; "crossmentship" is not a standard English word.

expectation: Run the full correction orchestrator. Remove the comparison because woodwork and wood work represent the same recognized compound in this context.
originalText: In central London, there is a old neighborhood called Holborn. It is known for its special pubs. One pub is Ye Olde Mitre. It has been open since the 1500s. It has small rooms and an outdoor area. It has been in famous movies. It shows history. Another pub is the Princess Louise. It was built in the late 1800s. It is known for its woodwork and old style features. Many visitors like to spend time in these pubs. They try traditional British drinks and food. The area is good for walking. Many pubs are close to each other. People can enjoy sightseeing and relax with a drink or food. For visitors staying nearby, the hotel The Hoxton offers a comfortable place to stay. It is a short walk from these old pubs.
userText: In central London there is a old neighbourhood called Holborn. It is known for  its special pubs. One pub is the Ye olde Mitre. It has been opened since the 1500's. It has small rooms and an outdoor area. It has been in famous movies, it shows history. Another pub is the Princess Louise, it was built in the late 1800's. It is known for its wood work and old style features. Many visitors like to spend time in these pubs. They try traditional British  drinks and food. The area is good for walking, many pubs are closed to each other. People can enjoy sightseeing and relax with a drink or food. For visitors staying nearby, the hotel The Hoxton offers a comfortable place to stay, it's a short walk from these old pubs.

expectedFinalComparisons.studentPhrase: The original uses the pub’s name as "Ye Olde Mitre" without "the" before "Ye," because "Ye Olde Mitre" is a proper name, not a regular noun phrase.
The original uses the adjective "open" after "has been" to describe a continuing state, while "opened" would describe the past action of starting to operate.
The original says "close to each other" to mean near in distance; "closed" usually means not open for business, which changes the meaning.
The original has the full verb "is," while your "s" looks like a cut-off or informal contraction and is not the correct written form here.

expectation: Run the full correction orchestrator. Remove the comparison because the year written with digits and number words has the same spoken value.
originalText: In Mount Isa, Ishani Harney makes cakes for birthdays, weddings, and events. She started her business in 2022. Many people like her cakes. But her shop has been vandalized many times in the past three years. The damage includes break-ins, graffiti, and a broken glass door. These events make her sad and cause money problems. She finds it hard to get help through grants. She tries to fix her shop, but she feels sad and thinks reporting vandalism might not help. The police tell shop owners to report damage. When they do not report, it is harder to find and stop the vandals.
userText: in mount Isa, ishani harney makes cakes for birthdays, weddings and events. she started her business in twenty twenty two. many people like her cakes but her shop has been vandalise many times in the past three years.  the damage include break in grafity and a broken glass door. this events make her sad and cost money problems. she find she hard to get a help. she tried to fix her shop but she feels sad and thinks reporting vandalism might not help. the police tell shop owner to report damage. where they do not report it is harder to find and stop the vandal.

expectedFinalComparisons.studentPhrase: The original uses the past participle "vandalized" for the passive form "has been vandalized," while "vandalise" is a base-form verb and also a different spelling.
"Includes" matches the singular subject "damage," needs the plural noun "break-ins," and "graffiti" is the correct spelling, while "include," "break in," and "grafity" are all incorrect here.
The original uses plural "These" to match "events," while "this" is singular and does not agree with the plural noun.
"Cause money problems" describes creating problems, while "cost money problems" is not a natural phrase and changes the meaning.
The original uses "finds it" (subject + verb + object), while "find she" is the wrong verb form and pronoun pattern in this structure.
"Help through grants" explains the type and source of help, while "a help" is missing this meaning and uses an article that does not fit here.
"Tries" is present tense to match the rest of the narrative, while "tried" shifts it unnecessarily to past tense.
"Owners" is plural to refer to many shop owners, while "owner" is singular and does not match the meaning.
"When they do not report" talks about the time or situation, while "where they do not report" incorrectly suggests a place.
"Vandals" is plural because there can be several people causing damage, while "vandal" is only one person.

expectation: Run the full correction orchestrator. Remove the comparison because the contraction and its expansion have the same meaning, and the comma is not graded.
originalText: Many people think paying off their student loans early is a smart move, but financial experts warn it can actually cost you a lot in the long run. A big financial company called Vanguard found that if you pay off low-interest debt early, you might miss out on important benefits like employer matching contributions to your retirement account. For example, some workers who clear their mortgages or student loans early are not taking full advantage of their employer’s 401(k) match. Over many years, missing that match can add up to hundreds of thousands of dollars less in your retirement savings. Experts say that missing out on these free contributions reduces your money growth over time and can lead to a significant shortfall when you retire. So, it’s important to consider your interest rates and the benefits of employer matches before paying off debt early.
userText: Many people think paying off their student loans early is a smart move. But financial experts warn it can actually cost you a lot in a long run. A big financial company called Vanguard found that if you pay off low interest at early you might miss out on an important benefits like employer matching contribution to retirement account. For example, some workers clear their or student loans early are not taking full advantages their employers 401k match, over many years, missing that match can adapt a hundred of thousands dollars lessen your retirement savings. Experts say that missing out on these free contributions reduces your money grow over time, and can lead to a significant short fall when you retire. So it is important to consider your interest rate and the benefit of employer matches before paying off debt early

expectedFinalComparisons.studentPhrase: The original uses "the long run" as a fixed expression; "a long run" sounds like one specific run, not the general idea over time.
The original uses the noun "debt" (money you owe), while "at" is just a preposition and does not fit the meaning here.
The original has "important benefits" without an article; adding "an" before "important" is ungrammatical with the plural noun "benefits".
"Contributions" is plural to match multiple payments, while "contribution" is singular and changes the meaning.
The original says "your retirement account"; dropping "your" removes the possessive meaning that the account belongs to you.
The original phrase is "workers who clear"; leaving out "who" makes the sentence less natural and grammatically incomplete.
The original has "mortgages or student loans"; omitting "mortgages" removes one of the types of debt being described.
The original uses the phrase "take advantage of"; changing it to "advantages" breaks the fixed expression and sounds incorrect.
The original writes "employer’s 401(k)" with a possessive apostrophe and parentheses, showing the plan belongs to the employer and using the standard format.
The original phrase "add up to hundreds" means gradually total a large amount, while "adapt a hundred" is not a correct or meaningful expression here.
The original uses the full phrase "hundreds of thousands of dollars," so you need the second "of" before "dollars."
In the original, "less in your retirement savings" uses "less" as an adjective, while "lessen" is a verb and does not fit this structure.
The original uses the noun "growth" after "money," while "grow" is a verb and cannot follow directly after "money" in this sentence.
"Shortfall" is one noun meaning a deficit, while "short fall" as two words suggests a brief drop and changes the meaning.
"Interest rates" is plural because it refers to different possible rates, while "interest rate" is singular.
"Benefits of employer matches" is plural because there are several advantages, while "benefit" is singular.

expectation: Run the full correction orchestrator. Keep daughters' versus daughters because the omitted possessive apostrophe changes the grammatical form, even though pronunciation is unchanged.
originalText: Jesy Nelson, a well-known singer, recently shared a heartfelt story about her twin daughters, Ocean Jade and Story Monroe. Just weeks before their diagnosis, Jesy noticed unusual signs in their movement and breathing, but she initially thought it was due to their prematurity. When they came home from the hospital, she and her mother observed that the babies were not moving their legs as they should and seemed to breathe from their bellies. Over time, these signs worsened, and Jesy realized her daughters might have a serious condition called Spinal Muscular Atrophy. After a diagnosis, she became an advocate, speaking openly about her daughters' health struggles and the importance of early detection. Her story highlights the challenges and emotional journey faced by parents of children with rare genetic disorders.
userText: Jessy Nelson a well know singer, recently shared a heart-felt story about her twin daughters, Ocean Jade and Story Monroe. Just weeks before their diagnosis Jessy noticed unusual signs in their movements and breathing but she initially thought it was due to their prematurity. When they came home from the hospital, she and her mother observed that the babies were not moving their legs as they should and seem to breathe from their bellies. Over time, this signs worsten and just she realized her daughters might have a serious condition called spinal muscular atrophy after a diagnosis she became an advocate speaking openly about her daughters health struggles and the importance of early detection. Her story highlights the challenges and emotional journey faced by parents of children with rare genetic disorders.

expectedFinalComparisons.studentPhrase: "Jesy" is a proper name with a fixed spelling, so writing "Jessy" changes the correct form of her name.
The original uses the past participle adjective "known" to describe the singer, while "know" is a base-form verb and does not work in this position.
Again, the singer’s name is spelled "Jesy" in the original, not "Jessy".
The original uses singular "movement" as an uncountable noun here, while "movements" makes it sound like separate countable actions.
"Seemed" is past tense to match the narrative, while "seem" is present/base form and breaks the past-time description.
The original has plural "these signs," so using singular "this" with plural "signs" is grammatically inconsistent.
"Worsened" is the correct past tense spelling; "worsten" is not a standard English form.
The original subject is the name "Jesy," but you wrote "just she," which changes both the word and the structure of the sentence.
The apostrophe in "daughters’" shows that the health struggles belong to the daughters, while "daughters" without an apostrophe does not mark possession.

expectation: Run the full correction orchestrator. Shrink the comparison to stock sale versus stocksale because stocksale is not an accepted compound; ignore punctuation, capitalization, and repeated spaces.
originalText: SpaceX is a rocket company led by Elon Musk. It wants to go public and have a big stock sale. They plan to raise seventy-five billion dollars by selling over half a million shares at one hundred thirty-five dollars each. This would beat the record of Saudi Aramco, which raised twenty-nine billion dollars. Musk will still control most of the company, holding about eighty-two percent of the voting shares. This is important because SpaceX is about to start trading on the stock market. Many investors and tech fans want to see how this big IPO will turn out.

expectedFinalComparisons.studentPhrase: The original uses the verb "is" ("SpaceX is a rocket company"), while "its" is a possessive pronoun and does not work as the main verb here.
The original uses "led" in the past tense, but "lead" is the base form and does not show that this leadership started in the past.
The original says "It wants" with third-person singular "-s", while "want" is missing this ending and is not correct for "it".
The original has two words "stock sale", but you wrote them together as "stocksale", which is not the standard form.
The original uses the plural "dollars" for seventy-five billion, while "dollar" is singular and does not match a large amount of money.

The original phrase is "thirty-five dollars each" (correct spelling and plural money unit), while "thrity five dollar age" changes the spelling, the plural form, and the word "each" into a different word.
The original uses the verb "beat" to show surpassing a record, but "be" is a different verb and does not express this meaning.
The original has only the preposition "of", while "by of" adds an extra preposition that makes the phrase ungrammatical.
The original uses "raised" in the past tense, but "raise" is present/base form and does not show the completed past action.
The original uses "percent" after a number, while "percents" is not standard in this context; we normally keep "percent" singular after numbers.
The original uses "will" to show a real expected future result, while "would" makes it sound more hypothetical or uncertain.

expectation: Run the full correction orchestrator. Shrink the comparison to the genuine connector error while excluding healthcare versus health care, which is an accepted compound variant.
originalText: Recent world politics have changed financial markets. As conflicts in the Middle East grow, the economy might change. When inflation goes up and growth slows, some stocks do better. Experts say investors should look at value stocks in energy, healthcare, and everyday goods. These areas did well in past high inflation times. With oil prices rising and inflation high, different companies might lead the market now. This change could create new chances for investors who watch carefully.
userText: Recent World Politics have changed financial market. As conflicts in the Middle East grow, the economy might change. When inflation goes up and growth slows, some stocks do better. Experts say investors should look at value stocks and energy, health care, and everyday goods. These areas did well in past high inflation times. With oil prices rising, inflation high, different companies might lead the market now. This change could create new chances for investors who watch carefully.

expectedFinalComparisons.studentPhrase: The original uses the plural "markets" because it refers to financial markets in general, not just one single market.
The original uses the preposition "in" to show which sectors to look at, while "and" incorrectly joins the list instead of linking it to "value stocks".
The original phrase is "and inflation," so leaving out "and" breaks the connection between oil prices rising and inflation being high.

expectation: Run the full correction orchestrator. Remove the comparison because sixty thousand and 60000 express the same complete quantity.
originalText: A new study shows that changing how parents introduce peanuts to babies has made a big difference. Since health officials in 2015 recommended giving infants peanut products starting at around four months, about sixty thousand children have avoided developing peanut allergies. Before that, doctors advised waiting until children were three years old, but the new guidance helped many kids stay allergy-free. Researchers from the Children's Hospital of Philadelphia looked at health records and found that peanut allergies in children under three dropped by more than a quarter after the new advice. They saw even bigger drops after the guidelines were expanded in 2017. Experts say this is a major success that has helped prevent serious food allergies in thousands of children.
userText: A new study shows that changing how parent introduce peanut to baby had made a big differences. Since health officials in 2015 recommended, giving peanut product storing around 4 months, about 60000 children have avoided deloping peanut allergies. Before that, doctor advice waiting until childrens were 3 years old, but the new guide in health many kids stay out allergy free. Researcher from children's hospital of philadephia looked at health records and found that peanut allergy in children under 3 dropped more than a quarter after a new advice. They saw even bigger drops after the guiline was expanded in 2017. Experts say it is a major success the help prevent serious food allergy in thousands of children

expectedFinalComparisons.studentPhrase: The original uses the plural "parents" (more than one parent), while "parent" is singular.
The original uses "peanuts" in the plural to talk about the food in general, not a single "peanut".
The original says "babies has" (plural noun with present perfect), while "baby had" changes both the number (singular) and the tense (past perfect instead of present perfect).
The original uses the singular "difference" to describe one main change, but "differences" suggests several changes.
The original phrase is "giving infants," so leaving out "infants" removes who is receiving the peanut products.
The original has "products starting at" (beginning at a time), while "product storing" changes both the noun number and the verb to a different meaning (keeping something, not beginning).
"Developing" is the correct spelling; "deloping" is a misspelling of the same word.
The original uses "doctors advised" (plural subject with past tense verb), while "doctor advice" changes to a singular noun and a noun form instead of the past tense verb.
"Children" is already plural, so adding "-s" to make "childrens" is incorrect.
The original phrase "guidance helped" means the advice produced a good result, while "guide in health" changes to a different noun phrase that does not work grammatically in this sentence.
The original just uses the verb "stay" before "allergy-free"; adding "out" changes the meaning and does not fit here.
The original uses plural "Researchers" and includes "the" before the institution, while your version has singular "Researcher" and omits "the".
"Philadelphia" is a proper noun and must be spelled exactly like this, not "philadephia".
The original says "peanut allergies" (more than one type or case), but you wrote the singular "allergy".
The original phrase is "dropped by more than a quarter"; your version misses the preposition "by" before "more".
The original uses "the new advice" to refer to specific advice already mentioned, not "a new advice".
The original has plural "guidelines were" with correct spelling, while you wrote singular "guiline was" with a spelling error and number disagreement.
The original uses "this" to point to the specific success just mentioned; "it" is less precise in this context.
The original uses the verb phrase "that has helped" (present perfect), but you wrote "the help," which is a noun phrase and does not show the action.
The original says "serious food allergies" (plural), but you wrote the singular "allergy."

expectation: Run the full correction orchestrator. Shrink the comparison to maintain versus mantain and exclude the equivalent punctuation and line-break differences around Summer.
originalText: As summer gets closer, many people think about changing their hair color, especially to blonde. Celebrity colorist Tauni Dawson, who works with famous celebrities, says that the popular shade this season is Hot Honey Blonde. It is a warm and rich color that looks natural and effortless. She recommends that if you want a brighter blonde, use special shampoos and conditioners to keep the color bright. For warmer blondes, it is better to avoid purple shampoo and instead use nourishing masks and water filters. Dawson says the best time to go blonde is when you feel ready and willing to take care of the color, not just because it is summer. She suggests starting with a softer, multi-dimensional shade like honey or caramel before going all the way to platinum. This makes the change look more natural and easier to maintain. Summer is a common time to try blonde hair because of the sun and the lively feel, but the most important thing is to pick a shade that fits your lifestyle and makes you feel confident.
userText:
As summer gets closer, many people think about changing their hair color, especially to blonde. 

Celebrity colorist Tauni Dawson, who works with famous celebrities, says that the popular shade this season is hot hunny blonde. It is a warm and rich color that looks natural end effortless. 

She recommends that if you want a brighter blonde, use especial shampoos and conditioners to keep the color bright. For warmer blondes it is better to avoid purple shampoo and instead use nourishing masks and  water filters. Dawson says the best time to go blonde is when you feel ready and willing to take care of the color, not just because it is summer. 

She suggests starting with a softer, multidimensional shade like honey or caramel before going all the way to platinum. This makes the change look more natural and easier to mantain. 

Summer is a common time to try blonde hair because of the sun and the light we feel. But the most important thing, is to pick a shade that fits your lifestyle and makes you feel confident.

expectedFinalComparisons.studentPhrase: "Honey" is the correct spelling; "hunny" is a common informal or incorrect variant and should not replace it in standard writing.
"And" is the correct conjunction here; "end" is a different word that means the final part of something.
In this context we say "special shampoos" for particular products, while "especial" is rare and sounds unnatural here.
"Maintain" is spelled with "tain" at the end; "mantain" is a common spelling mistake.
The original uses "the lively feel" to describe an energetic atmosphere, while "the light we feel" changes the meaning and is not a natural phrase here.

expectation: Run the full correction orchestrator. Split the comparison into London's versus London and system versus systems; exclude matching words, punctuation, and the line break.
originalText: In central London, there is an interesting area that many visitors overlook. The Inns of Court, such as Gray’s Inn and Lincoln’s Inn, are old places where lawyers learn and work. These sites are close to the Royal Courts of Justice and have impressive buildings, green gardens, and stories from long ago. A local guide offers a free walking tour that shows you these hidden places and explains their history and role in London's legal system. The tour begins near Temple station and goes past beautiful structures and quiet streets, giving you a look into a part of the city that has been there for many years. Before going, it is good to check the opening times because some places are only open at certain times. This walk is a great way for history fans and curious visitors to learn about London's special heritage.
userText:
In central London there is an interesting area that many visitors overlook, the Inns of Court such as Gray's Inn and Lincoln's Inn are all places where lawyers learn and work. These sides are close to the Royal courts of Justice and have impressive buildings, green gardens and stories from long ago. Our local guide offers a free walking tour that shows all these descent places and explains the history and role in London legal systems.
The tour begins near Temple station and goes past beautiful structures and quiet streets. Giving you a look into the part of the city that has been there for many years.
Before going, it is good to check the opening times because some places are only opened at certain times. This walk is a great way for history fans and curious visitors to learn about London special heritage.

expectedFinalComparisons.studentPhrase: "Old" describes the age of the places, while "all" means every one of them and changes the meaning of the sentence.
"Sites" means locations or places, but "sides" refers to surfaces or aspects and does not fit this context.
The original uses the article "A" to introduce one guide, while "Our" would suggest possession and change the meaning.
The original uses "you" to show who is being guided, while "all" incorrectly changes it to refer to the places instead of the person.
"Hidden" means not easily seen or known, while "descent" is unrelated in meaning and does not describe the places correctly.
"Their" shows that the history belongs to the places, while "the" is just a general article and loses the idea of possession.
"London’s" with an apostrophe shows the legal system belongs to London, while "London" alone is just the city name without possession.
The original uses singular "system" to talk about one legal system, while "systems" suggests more than one and changes the meaning.
"A part of the city" means one section among many, while "the part of the city" sounds like there is only one such part.
"Open" is the correct adjective after "are" here, while "opened" is a past-tense verb form and does not fit this structure.
"London's" is the possessive form showing the heritage belongs to London; "London" alone does not show this ownership.

expectation: Run the full correction orchestrator. Remove the comparison because Jr and junior are equivalent written forms of the same spoken title.
originalText: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. changed the schedule for giving vaccines to children. He did this without telling the CDC. He gave fewer vaccines, from seventeen to eleven. He took out vaccines for illnesses like flu and meningitis. He only kept them if a child is at high risk. He made the change quickly and without the usual science checks. Many experts were surprised. Kennedy is known for not trusting vaccines. He said he would not let his opinions change public health rules. But now, his actions seem to break that promise. Experts are worried about how his choices might affect children's health.
userText: robert f kennedy junior changed the schedule for giving vaccine the childern he did this without telling the CDC, he gave fewer vaccine from seventeen to eleven. he took up vaccine for illneses like flu and minungutus. he only kept them of the child that is high risk. he made the changed quickly and without the usual sign checks. many expert was surprised kennedy is known for not trusting vaccine. he said he would not let us opinion changed public health rules. but now, he is action seems break that promise. expert are worried about how his choices may affect childern health.

expectedFinalComparisons.studentPhrase: Use the plural "vaccines" (more than one) and spell "children" correctly, not "childern".
The original uses "vaccines" in the plural because more than one vaccine is given, not the singular "vaccine".
"Took out vaccines" means he removed several vaccines from the schedule, while "took up vaccine" suggests starting or accepting one vaccine and changes the meaning.
"Illnesses" is the correct spelling; "illneses" is a misspelling.
"Meningitis" is the correct medical term; "minungutus" is not an English word and is a mishearing and misspelling.
The original uses the conjunction "if a child" to show a condition, while "of the child" changes the grammar and meaning.
"Is at high risk" is the correct verb + preposition phrase; changing it to "that is high risk" alters the structure and sounds less natural here.
Here "change" is a noun (the change), so "made the change" is correct; "made the changed" uses the past verb form where a noun is needed.
"Science checks" refers to scientific reviews or tests, while "sign checks" would mean checking signs and does not fit this context.
With "many experts" you need the plural verb "were surprised", not the singular "expert was surprised".
"Vaccines" is plural in the original, but you wrote the singular "vaccine," which changes it from many vaccines to just one.
The original uses "his opinions change" (possessive pronoun + plural noun + base verb), but you wrote "us opinion changed," which uses the wrong pronoun, singular noun, and past tense verb.
The original phrase "his actions seem to" uses a possessive pronoun, plural noun, and base verb, while "he is action seems" mixes subject and possessive forms and repeats the verb incorrectly.
Review this correction carefully and compare the word form, meaning, and surrounding words.
"Might" and "may" are both modal verbs of possibility, but the original chooses "might" to show a slightly more uncertain possibility.
The original "children's" is the correct spelling and shows possession (the health of the children), while "childern" is misspelled and not possessive.

expectation: Run the full correction orchestrator. Remove the comparison because wanna is the informal spoken contraction of want to in this context.
originalText: Many people often have a song or tune in their head. They want to focus or relax, but the song stays. These short songs are called earworms. Many people get earworms at least once a week. Some get them every day. Listening to a song many times makes it more likely to become an earworm. Brain studies show that when we think about music, the same brain parts that hear real sounds become active. People with conditions like congenital amusia may feel music differently. Even though earworms go away, scientists find them interesting. They show how active and complex our brains are with music.
userText: many people often and have a song or tune in their head. they wanna focus or relax, but the songs stays. this short songs are called earworms. many people get earworms at least once a week some get them everyday. listening to a song many times makes it more  likely to become an earworm. brain studies show that when we think about music the same brain parts that hear real sounds become active. people with conditions like congenital amusia may feel music differently. even though earworms go away scienties find them interesting. they show how active and complex our brain are with music

expectedFinalComparisons.studentPhrase: The original just uses the verb "have" after "often"; adding "and" changes the structure and is not needed there.
The original uses the singular "song" to match the singular verb "stays"; changing it to "songs" makes the number disagree with the verb.
The original uses "These" to refer to plural "short songs," while "this" is singular and does not match the noun.
"Every day" (two words) is correct for how often something happens, while "everyday" (one word) is an adjective meaning ordinary.
The correct spelling is "scientists" for people who study science; "scienties" is not a standard English word.
The original uses plural "brains" to talk about people in general, so changing it to singular "brain" does not match that meaning.

expectation: Run the full correction orchestrator. Shrink the comparison to percent versus percents; ignore the harmless hyphen difference in eighty-two versus eighty two.
originalText: SpaceX is a rocket company led by Elon Musk. It wants to go public and have a big stock sale. They plan to raise seventy-five billion dollars by selling over half a million shares at one hundred thirty-five dollars each. This would beat the record of Saudi Aramco, which raised twenty-nine billion dollars. Musk will still control most of the company, holding about eighty-two percent of the voting shares. This is important because SpaceX is about to start trading on the stock market. Many investors and tech fans want to see how this big IPO will turn out.
userText: SpaceX its a rocket company lead by elon musk. it want to go public and have a big stocksale,  they plan to raise seventy five billion dollar by selling over half a million shares at one hundred thrity five dollar age. this would be the record by of saudi Aramco. which raise twenty nine billion dollars. musk will still control most of the company. holding about eighty two percents of the voting shares. this is important because SpaceX is about to start trading on the stock market. many investors and tech fans wanna see how this big IPO would turn out.

expectedFinalComparisons.studentPhrase: The original uses the verb "is" ("SpaceX is a rocket company"), while "its" is a possessive pronoun and does not work as the main verb here.
The original uses "led" in the past tense, but "lead" is the base form and does not show that this leadership started in the past.
"Wants" is third-person singular to match "It", while "want" is the base form and does not agree with the singular subject.
In the original, "stock sale" is two separate words; writing "stocksale" together is not the standard form.
The original uses the plural "dollars" for seventy-five billion, while "dollar" is singular and does not match a large amount of money.

The original phrase is "thirty-five dollars each" (correct spelling and plural money unit), while "thrity five dollar age" changes the spelling, the plural form, and the final word so the meaning is lost.
The original uses "beat" to show surpassing a record, but "be" is a different verb and does not express this comparison.
The original has only the preposition "of", while "by of" adds an extra preposition that makes the phrase ungrammatical.
"Raised" is past tense to describe what Saudi Aramco already did, while "raise" is present/base form and does not fit this past action.
"Percent" stays singular after a number ("eighty-two percent"), so adding an "s" in "percents" is incorrect in this context.
The original uses "will" to show a real expected future result, while "would" makes it sound more hypothetical or uncertain.

expectation: Run the full correction orchestrator. Shrink the comparison to sun versus the sun because the is extra spoken content; exclude aging versus ageing and matching without.
originalText: Fans of the game The Alters will like the new DLC called Last Variable. It will come out in July. The DLC has about twenty hours of new content. It has new ways to play and lets players build an underground base. Players can visit new places and try to make the planet green and lively. The story is about finding out the mystery of the Oasis. They get help from scientists like geologists and biologists, who have different ideas about science. While the main character sleeps in cryosleep, others on the planet have problems like aging without sun protection. The DLC adds more story and gameplay. It also has new survival challenges and scientific progress. A new trailer was shared before, showing what is coming.

It has new ways to play, let players build an underground base. players can visit new places and try to make the planet green and lively. The story is about finding out the mystery of the oases. They get help from scientists like geologists and biologists, who have different ideas about science, well the main character seems sincriously others on the planet have problems like ageing without the sun protection. The DLC adds more stories and game play. It also has new servival challenges and scientific progress. A new trailer was shared before showing what is coming.

expectedFinalComparisons.studentPhrase: "The Alters" is a game title (a proper noun), and "will like" is the future verb phrase, while "they outters well" changes both the name and the meaning.
The original uses "called" (past participle) after "DLC" to describe its name, but "call" is the base form and does not fit this pattern.
"Will" shows a planned future release, while "would" makes it sound hypothetical, so the modal verb changes the meaning.
The original phrase "and lets" needs the conjunction "and" plus "lets" with -s to match "it has", but "let" alone removes the link and changes the verb form.
"Oasis" here is a singular place name in the story, while "oases" is the regular plural form and does not match the original.
"While" introduces a time contrast between two actions, but "well" is an adverb of degree or filler and does not show this relationship.
"Sleeps in cryosleep" describes being in a frozen sleep state, while "seems sincriously" does not form a clear or correct English phrase with that meaning.
The original uses "sun" without an article in the fixed phrase "sun protection", so adding "the" changes the natural collocation.
"More story" means additional narrative as an uncountable idea, while "more stories" suggests several separate stories, which changes the meaning.
"Gameplay" is written as one word for how a game plays, while "game play" as two words is not the standard form here.
"Survival" is the correct spelling; "servival" is a spelling mistake with the same intended word.

expectation: Run the full correction orchestrator. Shrink the comparison to saw versus found; ignore the harmless diacritic difference in the proper name Mortvedt and the matching article after the range.
originalText: In Norway, a school trip led to a big find. A six-year-old boy called Henrik Refsnes Mørtvedt saw an old sword in the ground. Experts say the sword is about thirteen hundred years old from the Viking Age. It is made of iron and is different from most Viking swords. The sword was taken to the Museum of Cultural History in Oslo. Experts will clean and study it carefully. This find helps us learn about Norway's old history and shows why such discoveries are important.
userText: In Norway, a school trip led to a big find. A six year old boy called Henrik Refsnes Mortvedt found an old sword on the ground. Experts say the sword is about 1300 hundred years old from the Viking Age. It is made of iron and is different from most viking swords. The sword was taken to the Museum of Cultural History in Oslo. Experts were clean and study it carefully. This find helps us learned about Norway old history and shows  why  such discovery is/are important.

expectedFinalComparisons.studentPhrase: "Saw" means he noticed the sword with his eyes, while "found" suggests he discovered it, which changes the action in the story.
"In the ground" means the sword was inside the earth, while "on the ground" means it was lying on the surface, so the preposition changes the picture.
The original says "thirteen hundred years old" as words, while you wrote the number "1300," which is a different form from the spoken phrase.
"Will clean and study" uses the future modal "will" for a future action, but "were clean and study" is ungrammatical and does not show future meaning.
After "helps us," the verb should be base form "learn," not past tense "learned."
"Norway’s old history" uses the possessive form to show the history belongs to Norway, while "Norway old history" is missing that possessive.
The original uses plural "discoveries" with plural verb "are," but you changed it to singular "discovery is," which changes both number and verb form.

expectation: Run the full correction orchestrator. Remove the comparison because heatwave and heat wave are accepted spellings of the same spoken term.
originalText: Scientists work to save Ningaloo Reef in Australia. Last year, the reef lost many corals because of a long heatwave. The reef looked very sick. Scientists from the Western Australian Marine Science Institution started a project to help the reef. They made special structures called spawning cones. These cones collect coral larvae to help new corals grow. In April, a cyclone hit the area. Many people thought the coral would die. But the coral was strong and kept growing. This is good news for the reef. Scientists will watch the reef over the next few years. They want to see how well it heals from the heat and storms. They are hopeful that Ningaloo Reef will get better.
userText: Scientists  work to saved Ningaloo Reef in Australia.  Last year, the reef lost many corals because of a long heat wave. The reef looked very sick. Scientists from the Western Marine Science Institution started a project to help the reef. They made special structure called Spawning cones. This cones collect coral larvae to help new corals grow. In April, a cyclone hit the area. Many people thought the coral would die, but the coral was strong and kept growing. This is good news for the reef. Scientists will watch the reef over the next few years. They want to see how well it heals from the heat and storms. They are hopeful that Ningaloo Reef will get better.

expectedFinalComparisons.studentPhrase: The original uses the base form "save" after "work to," not the past tense "saved."
"Australian Marine" is part of the institution’s full name, so dropping "Australian" changes the proper noun.
The original uses the plural "structures" because they made more than one, not the singular "structure."
Because "cones" is plural, the original uses the plural demonstrative "These," not the singular "This."

expectation: Run the full correction orchestrator. Remove the comparison because hantavirus and Hanta virus are equivalent spellings of the same disease term in this context.
originalText: After six hard weeks in quarantine, one New Zealander and five Australians who were on the cruise ship MV Hondius are going home. The ship had a hantavirus outbreak, which is a disease from rodents. The passengers stayed in a special place near Perth, Australia. They were safe and healthy there. They tested negative for the virus the whole time. Australian Health Minister Mark Butler said they worked together and stayed positive. Their safe return makes everyone happy and shows people can work well during health problems.

The ship had a Hanta virus outbreak which is a disease from rodents. The passengers stayed in a special place near Perf, Australia. They were safe and healthy there. They tested negative for the virus the whole time. Australian Health Minister -Mark Butler said they work together and stayed positive. Their safe return makes everyone happy and shows people can work well during health problems.

expectedFinalComparisons.studentPhrase: The original uses "the cruise ship" to talk about a specific ship already known, while "a cruise ship" sounds like any cruise ship in general.
"MV" is part of the ship’s name and stands for "motor vessel," while "in the" is a separate phrase and not part of the proper noun.
"Perth" is the correct spelling of the Australian city name; "Perf" is a misspelling and not a place.
The original uses "worked" in past tense to match "stayed," but "work" is present/base form and does not fit the past-time description.

expectation: Run the full correction orchestrator. Remove the comparison because 512 gigabyte and 512 GB express the same value, and models is matching adjacent content in the full text.
originalText: Recently, gamers worldwide faced some tough news as Microsoft announced that the prices of its Xbox Series X consoles will go up starting early August. The company explained that rising costs for memory and storage are behind the increase, which will add between one hundred and fifty dollars to the price. The 512 gigabyte models will cost an extra hundred dollars, while the one terabyte versions will go up by one hundred and fifty dollars. The most expensive model with two terabytes of storage is being discontinued. This is not the first time prices have increased; Microsoft already raised them twice in the past year. Since the console was first introduced in twenty twenty, its price has grown by a quarter to nearly a third. The memory shortage affecting many electronics companies has made it more expensive to produce these devices, leading to higher costs for consumers. For fans, this means gaming is becoming more of a luxury, especially during the upcoming holiday season.

The company explained that raising costs for memory and storage are behind the increase, which will add between 150 dollars to the price. The 512 GB models will cost an extra 100 dollars, while 1 TB versions will go up by 150 dollars. The most expensive model with 2 TB of storage is being discontinued. This is not the first time prices have increased. Microsoft already rase up them twice in the last year. Since the console was first introduced in the 2020, its prices grown by a quarter, by nearly a thirtd, The memory shortage affecting many eletronics companies has made it more expensive to produce these devices, leaving a higher cost for consumers. For fans, this means gamming is becoming more of a luxuary, specially during the upcome holiday season

expectedFinalComparisons.studentPhrase: The original uses "faced" in past tense to match "Recently," while "face" is present tense and does not fit the time reference.
The original uses the possessive pronoun "its" to show the Xbox Series X belongs to Microsoft, while "the" is just an article and does not show ownership.
"Rising costs" is the natural phrase for prices that are going up, while "raising costs" would suggest someone is actively increasing them.
The original spells out "hundred" in words, while you wrote the numeral "100," so match the format you hear in the audio.
The original says "the one terabyte versions," while you wrote just "1," so you missed the word "one" as part of the full noun phrase.
The correct past form is "raised" and it does not take "up" here, so "rase up" is both the wrong spelling and an unnecessary extra word.
"Past year" is the phrase used in the original, while "last year" refers specifically to the previous calendar year and changes the time reference.
The original has "introduced in twenty twenty" without an article, so adding "the" before the year changes the structure.
The original uses singular "price has grown," but you wrote plural "prices grown," changing both number and the correct present perfect form.
The original says "grown by a quarter to nearly a third," where "to" shows the end point, while "by" would repeat the amount of change instead of the final level.
"Third" is the correct spelling; "thirtd" adds an unnecessary "d" at the end.
"Electronics" is spelled with "ctro" in the middle, not "etro" as in "eletronics".
The original "leading to" shows a result or consequence, while "leaving a" suggests something remains behind and changes the meaning.
"Costs" is plural in the original because there are many expenses, while "cost" is singular.
"Gaming" has only one "m"; "gamming" with double "mm" is a different word.
The original uses "luxury, especially"; "luxuary" is a spelling mistake and "specially" changes the meaning from "particularly" to "in a special way."
"Upcoming" is the correct form meaning "coming soon"; "upcome" is not standard English.
